restartStrategy.title: Restart strategy
restartStrategy.desc: Choose how instances are restarted. Each option affects scope and stability.

safe.title: Safe restart (recommended)
safe.principle: Send SIGTERM to the target instance processes so the app can save and exit, then relaunch that instance (force kill if needed).
safe.pros: Closer to a normal exit, less likely to trigger crash prompts, safer for data.
safe.cons: May take longer while waiting for a graceful shutdown.

force.title: Force restart
force.principle: Match processes by --user-data-dir and terminate them forcibly (SIGKILL / taskkill /F), then relaunch the target instance.
force.pros: Can precisely restart a single instance.
force.cons: May trigger “unexpected quit” prompts and risk losing unsaved data.

stop.title: Force close instance
stop.message: A termination signal (SIGTERM) will be sent to force close this instance, which may cause data loss. Continue?

delete.title: Delete Instance
delete.message: Delete instance {{name}}? This will remove the config and delete the instance folder.

verification.title: Account Verification

runHint.title: Verification notes
runHint.line1: Detect now sends a minimal chat request by simulating Antigravity with a local gateway and the official Language Server protocol.
runHint.line2: This consumes a small amount of tokens (usually one short dialogue). Actual usage depends on model and account policy.
runHint.line3: This operation does not modify wakeup task settings. It only checks account availability and writes detection records.
runHint.line4: If a 403 is returned, account verification or an appeal submission may be required. The result will include the URL and quick actions.

verification.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete?

wakeup.emptyDesc: Tasks will automatically trigger wakeups based on rules.

dialogs.riskWarning:
⚠️ Risk warning
Due to recent Google risk-control tightening, using the wakeup feature may increase the chance of triggering account risk controls (including but not limited to additional verification or temporary limits).
Please use it with caution based on your account status and evaluate the risk yourself.

Continue?

empty.description: Click "Add Account" to start managing your GitHub Copilot accounts

token.desc: Paste your GitHub Copilot access token or exported JSON data.

import.localDesc: Import GitHub Copilot account data from a JSON file.

githubCopilot.injectSuccess: Token injected to VS Code successfully. VS Code is launching.
githubCopilot.injectRequireClose: Please close VS Code before injecting.

flowNotice.title: GitHub Copilot account guide (click to expand/collapse)
flowNotice.desc: Switching accounts requires reading VS Code local auth storage and using the system credential service for decrypt/write-back. Data is processed locally only.
flowNotice.inject: One-click switching: This tool reads local VS Code encryption material (DPAPI/Keychain/Secret Service), decrypts the auth session in state.vscdb, replaces the GitHub Copilot token, and re-encrypts it — all locally on your machine.
flowNotice.reason: Permission scope: read VS Code auth database (state.vscdb) and call system credential capability (Windows DPAPI / macOS Keychain / Linux Secret Service) for decrypt/write-back.
flowNotice.storage: Data scope: only GitHub auth-session related entries are read/updated; all processes run locally.

summary.label: Overview
summary.title: Account Snapshot
summary.desc: See Antigravity and Codex at a glance

panels.emptyDesc: Add an account to see details

fileCorrupted.title: File Read Failed
fileCorrupted.description: File {{fileName}} is corrupted and cannot be parsed.
fileCorrupted.helpText: Please open the folder to repair manually or delete the file, then restart the application.

autoSwitch.title: Auto Switch
autoSwitch.thresholdDesc: Auto switch when any model quota falls below this percentage
autoSwitch.hint: Automatically switch to the account with the highest quota when any model quota is below the threshold.

quotaAlert.title: Quota Alert
quotaAlert.thresholdDesc: Trigger alert when any model quota falls below this percentage
quotaAlert.hint: When any current-account model quota drops below the threshold, send a native notification and show a quick-switch action in the app.

modal.title: Quota Alert
modal.desc: The current account quota has reached the alert threshold. Please handle it soon.

unsupported.desc: Windsurf multi-instance is available only on macOS.

empty.description: Click "Add Account" to start managing your Windsurf accounts

oauth.desc: Click the button below and complete Windsurf authorization in your browser.

token.desc: Paste your Windsurf access token or exported JSON data.

import.localDesc: Import Windsurf account data from a JSON file.

flowNotice.title: Windsurf account guide (click to expand/collapse)
flowNotice.desc: Switching accounts requires reading Windsurf local auth storage and, with client-compatible rules, using system credential/encryption capabilities for decrypt/write-back. Data is processed locally only.
flowNotice.inject: One-click switching: This tool reads local Windsurf encryption material (DPAPI/Keychain/Secret Service), processes the auth session in state.vscdb, writes back the Windsurf token, and keeps everything local on your machine.
flowNotice.reason: Permission scope: read Windsurf auth database (state.vscdb) and use system encryption capabilities (Windows DPAPI / macOS Keychain / Linux Secret Service) to decrypt and write back auth-session data.
flowNotice.storage: Data scope: only Windsurf auth-session related entries are read/updated; all processes run locally.

password.desc: Sign in with your Windsurf account email and password to automatically fetch API key and account info.

flowNotice.title: Kiro account guide (click to expand/collapse)
flowNotice.desc: Account switching reads and writes local Kiro auth files and instance settings on this device; OAuth login and quota refresh call Kiro official APIs.
flowNotice.reason: Processing paths include ~/.aws/sso/cache/kiro-auth-token.json, instance profile.json, and the kiro.kiroAgent entry in state.vscdb.
flowNotice.storage: Processed data includes Kiro session and quota information; requests to Kiro official APIs send required auth fields (such as code, refreshToken, accessToken, and profileArn).

token.desc: Paste your Kiro access token or exported JSON data.

breakout.serveHint: Press Space to launch the ball
breakout.closeConfirmDesc: Closing will end this run and progress will not be kept.

cautions.0: If you see abnormal statuses (for example 403/refresh failed), refresh first, then decide whether to remove and re-login.

outcomes.0: Supports OAuth, import, export, and bulk operations.
outcomes.1: Supports tag filtering, sorting, and grouped display.

steps.0: Click "Add Account" to complete authorization or import.
steps.1: Use "Refresh All" to sync one full round of quota status first.
steps.2: Sort by quota/reset time and choose the account you want to use now.

antigravity.summary: Manage the Antigravity account lifecycle: add, refresh quota, switch, group, and tag.
antigravity.title: Antigravity Account Management

cautions.0: Dashboard is for the big picture. For fine-grained operations, go to the specific platform page.

outcomes.0: See multi-platform "current account / recommended account / quota overview" on one page.
outcomes.1: Use quick switch buttons to reduce page-hopping cost.

steps.0: First check whether each platform card has account and quota data.
steps.1: If status is abnormal, click refresh or enter the corresponding platform page to handle it.
steps.2: If you need to change platform display order, open "Platform Layout" management.

dashboard.summary: For global overview: quickly check account status on each platform, use recommended switching, and jump to feature pages.
dashboard.title: Dashboard

cautions.0: Before importing JSON from untrusted sources, validate it in a test environment first.
cautions.1: Before deletion and bulk operations, confirm filters first to avoid accidental deletion.
cautions.2: Before sharing logs, redact sensitive data. Do not paste full token, cookie, email, etc.

outcomes.0: Import accounts in bulk for fast environment migration.
outcomes.1: Export JSON for backup or cross-device migration.
outcomes.2: Locate issues via error hints and file repair guidance.
outcomes.3: Quickly locate runtime issues through app.log* in Data Directory/logs.

steps.0: Before bulk operations, export current data once as a snapshot.
steps.1: On list pages, switch privacy view via "Show/Hide Email".
steps.2: When file corruption prompts appear, follow the dialog and open the directory to repair.
steps.3: For troubleshooting, go to "Settings -> Data Directory -> Open", then enter logs folder.
steps.4: Check the latest app.log or app.log.* first (date-rotated log files).
steps.5: When submitting feedback, include: occurrence time, platform, reproduction steps, key error logs (20 lines before/after).

dataPrivacy.summary: Covers daily maintenance: JSON import/export, masked email display, exception handling and recovery flow.
dataPrivacy.title: Import/Export, Privacy & Troubleshooting

cautions.0: Before deleting a fingerprint, check bound account count first to avoid affecting production accounts by mistake.

outcomes.0: Generate/capture new fingerprints and maintain metadata.
outcomes.1: View accounts bound to a fingerprint and add/remove bindings.

steps.1: Open details and confirm fingerprint info is correct.
steps.2: In binding management, bind accounts to target fingerprints.

fingerprints.summary: Manage fingerprint templates and account bindings. Supports generation, capture, import, and binding maintenance.
fingerprints.title: Device Fingerprints

cautions.0: Before copying from source instance, close the source first to avoid data inconsistency.
cautions.1: You cannot bind an account before a blank instance is initialized. This is expected behavior.

outcomes.1: Run multiple accounts in parallel to reduce repeated login switching.
outcomes.2: Validate new config in a test instance first, then return to main instance.

steps.0: When creating a new instance, prefer "Copy Source Instance" for faster usability.
steps.1: If you choose "Blank Instance", start once for initialization, then bind account.
steps.2: After binding account, manage lifecycle via "Start / Locate Window / Stop".

instances.summary: Used for account isolation and parallel running. Each instance has independent directory and state to avoid cross-contamination.
instances.title: Multi-Instance (Key)

cautions.0: Account management notes describe local read/write and permission scope. Read first before operating.
cautions.1: Some platforms or capabilities are system-limited. Follow on-page prompts.

keywords.0: Codex / GitHub Copilot / Windsurf / Kiro platforms

outcomes.1: GitHub Copilot/Windsurf/Kiro: support injection into VS Code chain.
outcomes.2: Each platform supports independent filtering, grouping, import, and export.

steps.0: Complete OAuth or Token import first.
steps.1: Confirm plan/quota and reset time are visible in account list.
steps.2: Execute "Switch/Inject" and verify in client that it takes effect.

providers.summary: The four platform pages share the same structure: account overview + multi-instance, with OAuth, Token/JSON import and switching.
providers.title: Codex / GitHub Copilot / Windsurf / Kiro Platforms

cautions.0: For first-time usage, operate one platform only. Finish one full flow before bulk import.
cautions.1: If a platform startup path is missing, fill path in settings before continuing.

outcomes.0: First check global status on Dashboard, then operate on specific platform pages.
outcomes.1: Complete one full closed loop on one platform first, then scale to others.
outcomes.2: Understand that "account management" and "multi-instance" are two different workflows.

steps.0: Open "Dashboard" and confirm the platforms you need are visible.
steps.1: Go to target platform page (for example Codex / GitHub Copilot), then add 1 account first.
steps.2: Use "Switch/Inject" button to verify account switching takes effect.
steps.3: Then go to "Multi-Instance" and create 1 instance to verify isolation and parallel running.

quickStart.summary: Follow the first-run sequence: "Check status -> Add account -> Switch account -> Create multi-instance" to avoid getting lost.
quickStart.title: Quick Start (5 minutes)

manual.searchPlaceholder: Search feature, scenario, or keyword (for example: multi-instance, inject, wakeup, fingerprint)

cautions.0: When path detection fails, manually selecting executable path is the safest option.
cautions.1: Too-low thresholds trigger frequent alerts. Start around 20%.

outcomes.0: Configure auto-refresh and quota alerts across platforms in one place.
outcomes.1: Set startup paths for each client and fix "missing path" issues.
outcomes.2: Adjust window behavior, language, and theme to fit your habits.

steps.0: In "General" tab, finish baseline language/theme/path setup first.
steps.1: Then adjust auto-refresh and alert threshold per platform.
steps.2: After network service port changes, restart as prompted to apply.

settings.summary: Centralized management for language, theme, auto-refresh, alert threshold, app paths, and network service settings.
settings.title: Settings & System Capabilities

manual.subtitle: Built-in instructions organized by task scenarios: know "why to use" first, then follow steps to "use immediately".
manual.title: User Manual

cautions.0: For first-time setup, select only a small number of accounts. Expand after stability is confirmed.
cautions.1: If verification result is abnormal, check errors and verification links in details first.

outcomes.0: Create recurring tasks and record historical execution results.
outcomes.1: Use verification page for batch checks and failure analysis.

steps.0: Create a new task in "Wakeup Tasks" and set model and trigger mode.
steps.1: Run manually once first to ensure task parameters are valid.
steps.2: On "Verification" page, run batch inspection and historical review.

wakeup.summary: Used for scheduled wakeup tasks and batch verification, helping continuous tracking of account availability and status.
wakeup.title: Wakeup Tasks & Verification
